headline: Congress hits pause for the long weekend—Senate in pro forma, House wraps up with a three-minute session!

senate.lastSessionSummary: The Senate convened in pro forma session for 30 seconds on Thursday afternoon, conducting no business. The chamber will return Monday to resume consideration of Second Circuit judicial nominee Matthew A. Schwartz and vote on Texas district judge nominee Arthur Roberts Jones.

onTheFloor.title: United States Circuit Judge for the Second Circuit
United States District Judge for the Southern District of Texas
onTheFloor.status: confirmation vote scheduled for 5:30 PM Monday

house.lastSessionSummary: The House met briefly at 3 p.m. and adjourned three minutes later without taking any votes. Thirty-nine public bills and three resolutions were introduced, and six bills with amendments were reported out of committee covering healthcare innovation, ALS therapy, family travel, homeland security, aviation security, and counterterrorism.

onTheFloor.title: Amend the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act to reauthorize through fiscal year 2031
Establish the IMPROVE Initiative within the National Institutes of Health
Reorganize and realign the Engagement, Liaison, and Outreach Office of the Department of Homeland Security
Establish funds for investments in aviation security
Assess terrorism threats from foreign terrorist organizations in major non-NATO ally countries

committees.topic: Hearings on Sunshine Protection Act, National Security appropriations, Veterans benefits, and Privacy in Purchases Act

committees.committee: Committee on Veterans' Affairs, Subcommittee on Technology Modernization
committees.topic: PACT Act Implementation and modernizing VA disability claims through technology
